steps.title: Set Up Both Cases for Absolute Value
steps.content:
The equation $|x - 5| = 10$ means $(x - 5)$ equals either $10$ or $-10$.
$$x - 5 = 10 \quad \text{or} \quad x - 5 = -10$$

steps.title: Solve Both Cases
steps.content:
Case 1: From $x - 5 = 10$, add 5 to both sides: $x = 15$.
Case 2: From $x - 5 = -10$, add 5 to both sides: $x = -5$.

Since the question asks for one possible solution, $x = 15$ is a valid answer.
